title: Parts of a Plant
subtitle: Learning What Each Part Does
subject: science

vocabulary.definition: The part of the plant that grows underground and absorbs water and nutrients.
The part that holds the plant upright and carries water from roots to leaves.
The flat green part that makes food for the plant using sunlight.
The part of the plant that produces seeds so new plants can grow.

activities.instruction: Match each plant part to its job.
Answer each question in your own words.
items: What would happen to a plant if it had no roots?
answers: It could not absorb water or stay anchored in the ground.

parentNotes: Try growing a bean sprout in a clear plastic bag so your child can watch the roots form. Takes about 5 to 7 days!
